threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot the newbies by their confusion at the bus routes; they pull out a map instead of checking the app like the rest of us.
Last night on the 47, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a keyboard app. Only in Bermondsey do you get live music at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The sound of the U-Bahn rattling beneath my feet during Sunday brunch made me realize this chaotic place felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find street art that doubles as a community fridge. Grab a snack while admiring the murals—only here!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can navigate the 14 Mission without needing Google Maps, all while timing their coffee run to avoid the morning rush.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 7 downtown today—three tourists tried to pet a dog on the tracks. The conductor's face was priceless. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think the outdoor terraces are for sipping drinks, but locals know they're just great spots for a quick siesta.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still try to order a 'café con leche' at 5 PM — locals know it's just 'café' after noon.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'fishtank' instead of a 'local pub' – everyone knows it’s the Cock in the middle of the high street.
Caught a glimpse of a wild pigeon nesting in the Night Bus's back seat. It seemed more at home than some of the passengers.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids using an old shopping cart as a makeshift go-kart on the sidewalk, I knew I was home. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous dance-offs in the middle of the street, especially near the Görlitzer Park entrance. It's a vibe you won't forget.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals don’t flinch at the sound of a helicopter; it’s just another part of city life. Tourists duck and stare.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 at Civic Center yesterday. Driver had to break for a pigeon staring down a tourist. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Park Güell thinking it's all Gaudí. They miss the real charm in the local taverns tucked away in the streets, serving honest pinchos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still carry a paid-for metro pass instead of getting a T-10. It’s all in the little details.
